summary: PR #2515 is focused match work in src/melee/lb/lbcollision.c for lb collision capsule/segment collision routines. The PR body reports four new 100% matches, four improved but still sub-100% functions, role-based parameter names for lbColl_80006E58, removal of an unused JObj matrix helper/assert strings, Vec3 grouping of coordinate triplets, an assignment-expression shape in the closest-points solve, and a float-register tactic that caches 1.0. Loaded bot feedback also reported an intermediate broken match in lbColl_80005C44 and the author said they would fix regressions, but the loaded slice does not include final regression output.

smart_moves.text: Role-based parameter names make a dense capsule-collision solve more reviewable while staying tied to the author’s stated confidence and the function’s collision roles.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text.md: author says they named fields they were pretty confident about
human_pr_text.md: lbColl_80006E58 role-name bullet

smart_moves.text: Converting repeated x/y/z scratch groups into Vec3 locals is a useful cleanup when the code is operating on geometric vectors and the diff shows equivalent grouped copies.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text.md: collapse float triplets into Vec3 bullet
raw_diff.diff: Vec3 c3/a2/c2/b0 locals replace scalar stack temporaries in branches

smart_moves.text: Removing a no-longer-referenced inline helper and its assert strings reduces stale source/data surface instead of preserving dead scaffolding.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text.md: Drop lbColl_JObjGetMtxPtr and assert strings; no longer referenced
raw_diff.diff: deleted helper and strings

smart_moves.text: The PR body explicitly separates new 100% matches from still-imperfect functions, avoiding overclaiming the large capsule routines.

decomp_lessons.text: For dense geometry/collision routines, semantic role names for endpoints, closest points, matrices, radii, and outputs can be justified when the function roles are clear; keep confidence bounded when the author notes remaining uncertainty.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text.md: role names for lbColl_80006E58
human_pr_text.md: author says named fields they were pretty confident about but more work likely needed

decomp_lessons.text: When scalar x/y/z temporaries form whole vector values, trying Vec3 locals/copies can improve readability and may help match shape, especially in parallel-axis collision branches.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text.md: collapse float xx_x/_y/_z triplets into Vec3 xx
raw_diff.diff: Vec3 locals replace scalar stack temporaries

decomp_lessons.text: Do not keep helper/assert scaffolding after a matching rewrite removes all references; deleting unused inline helpers and their private strings can be part of code-match cleanup when the PR evidence identifies them as unreferenced.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text.md: Drop lbColl_JObjGetMtxPtr and assert strings; no longer referenced
raw_diff.diff: deleted helper and strings

naming_conventions.text: Use role-pair names for collision capsule endpoints and closest-point outputs: hit_start/hit_end, hurt_start/hurt_end, hit_closest/hurt_closest.

naming_conventions.text: Use domain-specific radius and scale names when supported by collision math roles: hit_radius, hurt_radius, broadphase_scale.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Use role-pair parameter names in capsule collision routines when roles are clear
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2515 supports using names such as hit_start/hit_end, hurt_start/hurt_end, hit_closest/hurt_closest, out_contact_pos, out_overlap, hit_radius, hurt_radius, and broadphase_scale for lbColl_80006E58. The lesson is local and evidence-backed for this capsule-collision solve, not a blanket naming rule.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text.md: lbColl_80006E58 role names
raw_diff.diff: lbColl_80006E58 prototype

accepted_candidate_records.title: Try Vec3 locals when scalar x/y/z temporaries are acting as a vector
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2515 states and the diff illustrates that collapsing float xx_x/_y/_z triplets into Vec3 locals was part of improving lbcollision matching/readability. This is most supported for lbColl_80005C44 and parallel-axis collision branches.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text.md: Vec3 triplet collapse bullet
raw_diff.diff: Vec3 c3/a2/c2/b0 and other grouped vector locals

accepted_candidate_records.title: Record residual float-register allocation debt separately from structural progress
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2515 reports large percentage improvements while explicitly noting that three remaining sub-100% functions still have residual float-register-allocation mismatches. This is useful bookkeeping for later match work.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text.md: residual float-register-allocation mismatches

source_update_candidates.title: Consider documenting Vec3 grouping as a typed-fields/vector-structure matching cleanup pattern
source_update_candidates.text: This PR provides a concrete example where scalar coordinate triplets were collapsed into Vec3 locals in collision math. A curator may consider whether existing standards should explicitly mention using known math/vector types for x/y/z scratch groups when evidence supports the grouped value.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text.md: collapse float xx_x/_y/_z triplets into Vec3 xx
raw_diff.diff: Vec3 locals replace scalar temporaries
source_update_candidates.reason: This overlaps with typed-fields-over-pointer-math and infer-authored-source-style, but the current standards do not explicitly call out vector triplet grouping.

source_update_candidates.title: Consider adding a cautionary note for float-register-allocation tactics
source_update_candidates.text: The PR body names float-register-allocation mismatches and a tactic of caching 1.0 in a callee-save float. Curator may decide whether matching-tactics guidance should include examples for float constant caching/register allocation, with required objdiff/regression evidence.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text.md: cache 1.0 in a callee-save float
human_pr_text.md: residual float-register-allocation mismatches
source_update_candidates.reason: Current standards cover broad tactics needing evidence; this PR offers a searchable example but not enough evidence to promote a universal tactic.

rejection_notes: Do not promote the intermediate bot report as final validation because it included a broken lbColl_80005C44 match and no later bot report is loaded.
Do not infer reviewer intent or acceptance rationale; no reviews or review comments are present.
Do not generalize PAD_STACK increases as acceptable style from this PR alone; the loaded evidence shows use but not narrow objdiff/regression justification.
Do not promote all renamed fields as globally proven semantic names; the author explicitly framed some naming as confident but still likely needing future work.
